rationale: The citing paper references Chernev and Hamilton (2009) only in the literature review as one prior store-choice finding, noting a concave link between assortment size and store attractiveness. The citation helps map the general background on assortment and store choice, but the paper does not build its hypotheses, model, measures, or experiments directly on that study. The core mechanism in the citing paper centers favorite-brand availability, substitution, lock-in, and return behavior, and Chernev and Hamilton is not used again in a distinct analytical role.
